title: holcus sorghum subsp. sudanensis
intro: Holcus sorghum subsp. sudanensis, also known as Sudan grass, is a subspecies of sorghum grass that is widely cultivated for its edible seeds, forage, and hay. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its content of sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic compounds. Consuming Sudan grass has been associated with various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and exhibiting antioxidant and anti-diabetic properties. Herbal preparations of Sudan grass include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
